rule: Skills steer agent behavior and proportionality; the CLI is preferred for robust state inspection, mutation, validation, and reporting when it is available.

specs.title: Startup Router Skill
applies_when: An agent begins or resumes work in a repository before opening broad workspace state.
The task shape, active planning state, or applicable package skill is not yet known.

artifact_creation_allowed_when: Compact routing reports lane, epic, high-assurance, or scope-widened work that requires durable planning ownership.
The task cannot be completed honestly without preserving continuation state.
proportionality.ordinary_work_rule: Direct low-risk work may proceed from compact startup guidance and local proof without creating planning, review, or memory artifacts.

preferred_cli_commands.purpose: Resolve work shape, active state, skill routing, next-safe-action, proof hints, and proportional read budget before raw workspace reads.
decisions_answered: which package skill or fallback path should guide the next action
which exact detail command should be used before opening larger surfaces
preferred_cli_commands.failure_fallback: Read the installed workflow instructions only far enough to identify the effective CLI invocation and conservative no-CLI startup path.

interpreted_output_fields.decision: The next allowed action before any broad file reads.
The skill guidance to load when task-specific instructions are useful.
interpreted_output_fields.fallback_when_missing: Use the workflow prose as a conservative fallback and avoid durable artifact creation unless the work shape requires it.

interpreted_output_fields.decision: Whether implementation can start without additional planning or proof setup.
interpreted_output_fields.fallback_when_missing: Treat implementation as provisional and inspect the compact planning safety gate before editing.

owned_surfaces.edit_rule: Read effective CLI invocation and workflow policy; do not mutate during startup routing.
Use only as fallback when the CLI is unavailable.

allowed_actions: Run the configured startup CLI command before broad workspace inspection.
Use selector or detail commands when compact output identifies the needed field.
Proceed with direct work when compact routing permits it and proof is obvious.
forbidden_actions: Open raw planning or memory state before compact startup routing points there.
Create planning, review, memory, or handoff artifacts for routine direct work.
Treat generated skill or adapter output as the source of product behavior.
fallback_when_cli_unavailable: Use the smallest safe manual path and preserve no-artifact-by-default behavior.
Escalate to planning only when the work shape, risk, or continuation need requires durable state.
proof_obligations: Use the proof command or compact proof hints before claiming completion.
For direct work, name the narrow validation or inspection that proves the change.
closeout_obligations: Report delivered behavior, proof, and any deferred scope.
Do not claim completion when compact routing reports closure blockers or missing continuation ownership.

must_preserve: No-artifact-by-default behavior for ordinary direct work.
Forbidden raw planning/memory reads before compact routing.
generated_target_requirements.deferred_until: A renderer slice is intentionally selected after the SkillSpec contract proves sufficient.

specs.title: Planning Autopilot Skill
applies_when: Compact startup or summary output reports active planning, lane/epic work, scope growth, or a planning mutation next action.
An agent needs to create, promote, update, or close durable planning state.

artifact_creation_allowed_when: The CLI reports planning-backed work, active execution pressure, or a required planning safety gate.
The work has grown beyond a direct slice and needs durable continuation ownership.
proportionality.ordinary_work_rule: Use planning only when the router requires it; do not route routine direct edits into reviews or execplans.

preferred_cli_commands.purpose: Inspect active planning state, active execplans, planning surface health, and recommended next action without opening raw state first.
decisions_answered: whether an active planning item already owns the work
whether raw planning detail needs to be opened
which planning mutation or validation should happen next
preferred_cli_commands.failure_fallback: Read the planning workflow guidance first, then inspect only the named active state surface needed to continue safely.

preferred_cli_commands.preferred_invocation: uv run agentic-workspace planning promote-to-plan --item-id <id> --target . --format json
preferred_cli_commands.purpose: Move an accepted planning candidate into an execplan while preserving planning provenance.
preferred_cli_commands.failure_fallback: Do not hand-edit state; use planning recovery or preflight guidance before attempting manual repair.

preferred_cli_commands.preferred_invocation: uv run agentic-workspace planning delegation-decision --route keep-local --skipped-reason <reason> --target . --format json
preferred_cli_commands.purpose: Record why a lane, high-assurance, or decomposed slice is being kept local or delegated.
decisions_answered: whether high-assurance planning has explicit execution ownership
whether implementation can proceed without hiding delegation obligations
preferred_cli_commands.failure_fallback: Pause implementation and inspect preflight or planning doctor output rather than bypassing the planning gate.

interpreted_output_fields.decision: The active planning item that owns the current work.
The checked-in execplan to inspect when implementation needs plan detail.

interpreted_output_fields.fallback_when_missing: Run planning doctor or preflight before mutating state.

owned_surfaces.edit_rule: Mutate through planning CLI commands when available.
Create, promote, update, archive, or close through planning CLI commands when available.

allowed_actions: Use summary, planning mutation commands, and proof commands as the primary state interface.
Open the active execplan only after compact output points there.
Record keep-local or delegation decisions for lane/high-assurance work before implementation.
forbidden_actions: Hand-edit planning state when a planning CLI mutation command is available.
Create review artifacts as issue intake or active planning substitutes.
Close planning work without proof, continuation, and acceptance reconciliation.
fallback_when_cli_unavailable: Read planning workflow guidance before raw state edits.
Prefer recovery/preflight instructions over ad hoc state repair.
Leave a minimal continuation note only when durable state cannot be safely mutated through the package.
proof_obligations: Run changed-path proof selected by `agentic-workspace implement` or `agentic-workspace proof`.
Verify planning surface health before closeout.
closeout_obligations: Archive or close only the planning item that the slice actually completed.
Keep codegen or generated target work deferred unless the selected slice explicitly owns it.

must_preserve: Planning CLI commands own state mutation when available.
Issue intake belongs in planning state, not review artifacts.
Routine work does not create planning artifacts by default.
Codegen and target renderer work remain separately selectable follow-ons.
generated_target_requirements.deferred_until: Generated skill rendering is selected as its own codegen slice.
